title: Publisher Confirms, Windows, and Ambiguity · Language Lens: Confirm Queue Semantics
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must enable confirms before publishing, drain confirmation channels, bound outstanding sequence numbers, and reconcile close ambiguity; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the confirm queue semantics increment in a bounded Go confirm-window ledger. Learners progr…
objectives: Explain when confirms occur for unroutable messages, persistent classic queues, quorum queues, and multiple routed queues; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
